hypothesis: Training direct-force NNIPs with an added differentiable regularization term proportional to the Force Delta (RMS disagreement between conservative and non-conservative forces) will produce models that have lower true RMS force error on adversarially generated out-of-distribution structures and a significantly stronger correlation (higher Spearman rank and Pearson) between U∆ and εdirect at test time than identical models trained without this regularizer.
